judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both nail all four points; Report 2 is marginally more rigorous by checking freshness against origin/main with an explicit ancestry test.

report_1.evidence: **1. Base branch: `main`.** ... There is no reason to target the now-merged `feat/weighted-scorer`; targeting it would leave the PR pointed at a stale, deletable branch.

report_2.evidence: **1. Base branch: `main`.** The only other candidate base, `feat/weighted-scorer`, is a dead base ... Targeting `feat/weighted-scorer` would stack the PR on a branch that is already merged under a different SHA; a PR against `main` is correct.

report_1.evidence: was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` ... That starting point has since been **squash-merged into `main`**: `main` has a single commit `1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"` ... Because it was a squash merge, commits W1–W3 are *not* ancestors of `main`

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` ... was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (`ac9d505`) ... `feat/weighted-scorer` is **not an ancestor of main** ... yet its full content **is already in main** as the single squash commit `1971f08` (PR #42) ... the branch still exists on the remote, but it is already merged in squashed form.

report_1.evidence: `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` touches `prompts/grader.txt` (+1), `scoring/metrics.py` (+10), `scoring/scorer.py` (+13/−3). Not all of it is pairwise work: ... the whole weighted rewrite of `scoring/scorer.py`. These are the W1–W3 weighted-scorer changes, present because the merge-base is the root commit `65204ad` — the squash merge of #42 left W1–W3 outside `main`'s history, so the PR view re-shows their content

report_2.evidence: the three-dot diff (`origin/main...feat/pairwise-metric`) shows 21 insertions across `prompts/grader.txt`, `scoring/metrics.py`, and `scoring/scorer.py` — so a naive PR would *appear* to re-introduce the weighted-scorer changes ... W1–W3 are present only because the branch was stacked on `feat/weighted-scorer` before that branch was squash-merged into main as `1971f08`

report_1.evidence: Rebase only the pairwise commits onto `main`, skipping the already-merged W1–W3: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... Verify the result: history should be `65204ad → 1971f08 → X1' → X2'`, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now show only the 5 pairwise lines.

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... Verify the result: branch contains the current main tip (`git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main feat/pairwise-metric`), and `git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline` shows only the two X commits with the same 5-line diff.
